title: Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

narrative_moves.purpose: Explain why unsupervised multivariate time-series anomaly detection matters.
Show that reconstruction error alone misses relationship-level anomaly signals.
Turn the difference between prior and learned associations into an anomaly criterion.
Use minimax learning to amplify normal/anomalous association differences.
Compare against representative baselines on benchmark datasets.
Test whether association discrepancy and training strategy contribute independently.
Make learned temporal associations and anomaly contrast inspectable.
Limit conclusions to evaluated datasets and protocol.

claims.statement: Anomalies can be characterized by temporal association patterns rather than only point-wise reconstruction error.
Association discrepancy supplies an anomaly-sensitive signal and minimax learning sharpens it.
The reported method improves benchmark detection results under the paper's evaluation protocol.
The evidence is limited to the datasets, anomaly labels, preprocessing and point-adjustment protocol used in the study.

experiment_intents.title: Main benchmark comparison
experiment_intents.question: Does the complete method outperform representative alternatives across datasets?
experiment_intents.design_reason: Multiple public datasets and baseline families test whether gains generalize beyond one operational setting.

experiment_intents.title: Mechanism ablation
experiment_intents.question: Which components are necessary for the observed gain?
experiment_intents.design_reason: Removing discrepancy or minimax elements separates the claimed mechanism from model capacity.

limitations: This reconstructed record is a single-reviewer development seed, not a frozen Gold item.
Page numbers and exact quotations are withheld until an authorized full text is parsed and reviewed.
Reported comparisons should be re-evaluated under consistent, non-inflating anomaly scoring protocols.
